title: HGETDEL
description: Returns the value of a field and deletes it from the hash.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Get and delete the value of one or more fields of a given hash key. When the last field is deleted, the key will also be deleted. ## Example ``` redis> HSET mykey field1 "Hello" field2 "World" field3 "!" (integer) 3 redis> HGETALL mykey 1) "field1" 2) "Hello" 3) "field2" 4) "World" 5) "field3" 6) "!" redis> HGETDEL mykey FIELDS 2 field3 field4 1) "!" 2) (nil) redis> HGETALL mykey 1) "field1" 2) "Hello" 3) "field2" 4) "World" redis> HGETDEL mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2 1) "Hello" 2) "World" redis> KEYS * (empty array) ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of deleted fields and their values or `nil` for fields that do not exist. **RESP3:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of deleted fields and their values or `nil` for fields that do not exist.
intro: Get and delete the value of one or more fields of a given hash key. When the last field is deleted, the key will also be deleted.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Get and delete the value of one or more fields of a given hash key. When the last field is deleted, the key will also be deleted.

sections.title: Example
sections.content:
```
redis> HSET mykey field1 "Hello" field2 "World" field3 "!"
(integer) 3
redis> HGETALL mykey
1) "field1"
2) "Hello"
3) "field2"
4) "World"
5) "field3"
6) "!"
redis> HGETDEL mykey FIELDS 2 field3 field4
1) "!"
2) (nil)
redis> HGETALL mykey
1) "field1"
2) "Hello"
3) "field2"
4) "World"
redis> HGETDEL mykey FIELDS 2 field1 field2
1) "Hello"
2) "World"
redis> KEYS *
(empty array)
```

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of deleted fields and their values or `nil` for fields that do not exist.

**RESP3:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of deleted fields and their values or `nil` for fields that do not exist.
